wegovy_coverage: Covered via 9amHealth weight management program for clinically eligible employees and adult dependents (BMI >=35 with two or more qualifying conditions); copays/cost-shares apply per medical plan
zepbound_coverage: Covered via 9amHealth weight management program for clinically eligible employees and adult dependents (BMI >=35 with two or more qualifying conditions); copays/cost-shares apply per medical plan
ozempic_coverage: Coverage for diabetes indication via Amazon medical plans (Aetna/Premera) — formulary specifics not publicly disclosed; prior auth applies
mounjaro_coverage: Coverage for diabetes indication via Amazon medical plans (Aetna/Premera) — formulary specifics not publicly disclosed; prior auth applies
notes: VERIFIED 2026-04-07 via primary-vendor source: 9amHealth Amazon program page (https://join9am.com/amazon). Effective Jan 1, 2026, Amazon expanded eligibility for its 9amHealth weight management benefit. Per the program page, employees with BMI >=30 receive lifestyle management support, and employees with BMI >=35 PLUS two or more qualifying medical conditions are eligible to receive GLP-1 medications (in addition to other services) through 9amHealth. Active engagement is defined as four weigh-ins and four app engagements per rolling 30 days for those receiving GLP-1 medications. Copays/cost-shares for medications, lab tests, devices, bariatric surgery, and behavioral health support may apply. Employees who don't qualify for 9amHealth can find additional weight management resources through A to Z Benefits or by contacting MyHR. Diabetes-indication GLP-1 (Ozempic, Mounjaro) coverage flows through Amazon's standard medical plans (Aetna/Premera) with prior authorization, but specific formulary tier/copay details are not publicly disclosed.

wegovy_coverage: Covered for clinically eligible members via Calibrate program (copay varies by plan)
zepbound_coverage: Covered for clinically eligible members via Calibrate program (copay varies by plan)
notes: VERIFIED via primary sources: Optum Rx FedEx member portal (https://welcome.optumrx.com/fedex/landing) and the Optum Rx 'Calibrate Program' benefit document for FedEx (https://contenthub-aem.optumrx.com/content/dam/contenthub/rx-assets/en/documents/clients/fedex/fedex_oe/Calibrate-Program.pdf), plus Calibrate's FedEx benefit pages (https://www.joincalibrate.com/pages/fedex and https://www.joincalibrate.com/pages/fedexpilot). FedEx team members access GLP-1 weight-loss medications (Wegovy, Zepbound, Saxenda) for clinically eligible members through the Calibrate program, administered through FedEx's pharmacy benefit plan managed by Optum Rx; copays depend on the medical plan. Calibrate is a fully covered benefit for FedEx pilots. Members reach the program via Choose Well Care Connect at choosewell.fedex.com or 1-833-FDX-WELL.

ozempic_coverage: Covered ($0 copay if for diabetes via preventive list)
mounjaro_coverage: Covered ($0 copay if for diabetes via preventive list)
notes: VERIFIED via primary source: JPMorgan Chase 2026 U.S. Annual Benefits Enrollment Bulletin (https://myhealth.jpmorganchase.com/api/office365/1.0/downloadFile/2026_JPMC_Enrollment_Bulletin_National.pdf?appType=MH). Per the bulletin, effective Jan 1, 2026 GLP-1 anti-obesity medications such as Wegovy are covered at a $50/month copay under Medical Plan Option 1 and $100/month under Option 2 via CVS Caremark, following Preferred Brand Name plan design. GLP-1s FDA-indicated for diabetes remain on the preventive drug list at $0 copay. Prior authorization applies.

zepbound_coverage: Covered by some carriers (prior auth, varies)
notes: VERIFIED via primary sources: OPM 2023 carrier guidance requires every FEHB carrier to cover at least one GLP-1 anti-obesity drug (https://www.opm.gov/healthcare-insurance/healthcare/carriers/) and Checkbook's FEHB plan comparison (https://help.checkbook.org/article/223-weight-loss-drug-coverage-from-fehb-plans). Effective Jan 1, 2025 BCBS FEP downgraded Wegovy/Zepbound to a higher tier with out-of-pocket cost increases of $600-$700/mo (CNN, https://www.cnn.com/2025/07/01/health/zepbound-wegovy-insurance-cvs-bcbs-weight-loss). CVS Caremark removed Zepbound and made Wegovy preferred effective July 1, 2025. Coverage and tier vary by carrier and plan year; verify in your specific FEHB plan brochure on opm.gov.

zepbound_coverage: Covered by some carriers (prior auth, varies)
notes: VERIFIED via primary source: OPM PSHB program (https://www.opm.gov/healthcare-insurance/pshb/) launched Jan 1, 2025 and requires PSHB plan benefits to be equivalent to FEHB benefits, which means each carrier must cover at least one GLP-1 anti-obesity drug. Coverage specifics mirror the equivalent FEHB plan; verify in your PSHB plan brochure. Source: Government Executive coverage of PSHB Rx benefits (https://www.govexec.com/pay-benefits/2024/02/postal-service-health-benefits-program-prescription-coverage-and-open-season-2025/394298/).

wegovy_coverage: Fully covered via Calibrate program for Brookfield employees on Aetna PPO, Aetna EPO, or Aetna HDHP plans (no deductible required)
zepbound_coverage: Coverage available via Calibrate program when clinically appropriate; verify current formulary with Calibrate enrollment
ozempic_coverage: Diabetes-indication coverage flows through Brookfield's standard medical/pharmacy plan; verify with HR
mounjaro_coverage: Diabetes-indication coverage flows through Brookfield's standard medical/pharmacy plan; verify with HR
notes: VERIFIED 2026-04-08 via primary-vendor source: Calibrate Brookfield benefit page (https://www.joincalibrate.com/pages/brookfield), confirmed by direct WebFetch. Per the page: 'If you have an Aetna PPO, an Aetna EPO or an Aetna HDHP plan, your GLP-1 medication will be fully covered' and 'You do not have to meet your deductible — if you have one — in order for your GLP-1 medication to be covered.' Program includes GLP-1 medications (Wegovy, Saxenda) plus lifestyle coaching. Coverage applies only to Brookfield employees enrolled in qualifying Aetna plans; non-Aetna or non-clinically-eligible employees are out of scope.

zepbound_coverage: Coverage available via Calibrate program when clinically appropriate; verify current formulary with Calibrate enrollment
ozempic_coverage: Diabetes-indication coverage flows through Paychex's standard medical/pharmacy plan; verify with HR
mounjaro_coverage: Diabetes-indication coverage flows through Paychex's standard medical/pharmacy plan; verify with HR

wegovy_coverage: Fully covered via Calibrate program for clinically eligible ManpowerGroup employees and dependents
zepbound_coverage: Coverage available via Calibrate program when clinically appropriate; verify current formulary with Calibrate enrollment
ozempic_coverage: Diabetes-indication coverage flows through ManpowerGroup's standard medical/pharmacy plan; verify with HR
mounjaro_coverage: Diabetes-indication coverage flows through ManpowerGroup's standard medical/pharmacy plan; verify with HR
notes: VERIFIED 2026-04-08 via primary-vendor source: Calibrate ManpowerGroup benefit page (https://www.joincalibrate.com/pages/manpowergroup). Per the Calibrate page, the program is a fully covered benefit for ManpowerGroup employees and includes GLP-1 medications plus lifestyle coaching, with reported average 18% weight loss. Coverage applies only to clinically eligible members; weight-management coverage details for non-Calibrate-routed care are not publicly disclosed.

wegovy_coverage: Coverage requires Microsoft to elect the weight-management drug benefit under Premera medical policy 5.01.621. As of 2024-2025 verification, Wegovy is non-formulary for weight loss specifically; the cardiovascular indication (FDA March 2024) is covered under different criteria. Patients must verify current Microsoft plan election with HR.
zepbound_coverage: Same as Wegovy: coverage available only if Microsoft has elected the weight-management drug benefit. Verify current election with HR.
ozempic_coverage: Covered for diabetes diagnosis with prior authorization, per standard Premera formularies.
mounjaro_coverage: Coverage for diabetes diagnosis with prior authorization. Weight-management indication (Zepbound) follows the weight-management benefit election.
notes: Re-verified 2026-04-08: Microsoft uses Premera BCBS as plan administrator. The relevant policy is Premera Medical Policy 5.01.621 (https://www.premera.com/medicalpolicies/5.01.621.pdf), which confirms weight-management GLP-1s are covered only for employer groups that have explicitly elected the benefit. Premera's preventive drug list (https://www.premera.com/documents/022506.pdf) is publicly indexed but does not confirm anti-obesity GLP-1 coverage as part of Microsoft's elected benefit. Per the 2024 Premera provider news (https://providernews.premera.com/wegovy-coverage-with-the-new-cardiovascular-indication/), the FDA March 2024 cardiovascular indication for Wegovy created a separate coverage pathway. Microsoft's specific weight-management benefit election status as of 2026 cannot be independently confirmed from publicly indexed sources; patients should verify directly with HR. Confidence is medium because the plan administrator and policy framework are confirmed but the specific election decision is not.
